content.problemStatement:
You are given a 111-indexed string SSS of length NNN containing lowercase English letters.

You also have an initially empty string AAA.

Repeat the following process until SSS is empty:


Find the lexicographically maximal substring of SSS;
Let it be S[l..r]S[l..r]S[l..r], where 1≤l≤r≤∣S∣1 \le l \le r \le |S|1≤l≤r≤∣S∣ and ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ denotes the current length of SSS (if there are multiple occurrences of the maximal substring, you may choose any of them);
Append this substring to AAA, i.e. set A:=A+S[l..r]A := A + S[l..r]A:=A+S[l..r], where +++ denotes string concatenation;
Delete S[l..r]S[l..r]S[l..r] from SSS and concatenate the remaining parts, i.e. set S:=S[1..l−1]+s[r+1..∣s∣]S := S[1..l-1] + s[r+1..|s|]S:=S[1..l−1]+s[r+1..∣s∣] (note that one or both of these substrings may be empty).
Output the value of AAA at the end of the process.

It can be proved that the output is unique.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains a lowercase English string SSS of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the value of string AAA at the end of the process.
constraints: SSS consists of lowercase English letters, i.e, the characters `a` through `z`.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The process takes three steps:

The lexicographically maximal substring of "abacaba" is "caba" with l=4l = 4l=4, r=7r = 7r=7.
AAA becomes "caba", SSS becomes "aba".
The lexicographically maximal substring is "ba" with l=2l = 2l=2, r=3r = 3r=3.
AAA becomes "cababa", SSS becomes "a".
The lexicographically maximal substring is "a" with l=1l = 1l=1, r=1r = 1r=1.
AAA becomes "cababaa", SSS becomes empty.

The final value of ttt is "cababaa".
